coveredRequirements: worktree-cleanup without args lists available worktrees via git and presents interactive selection
worktree-cleanup with an explicit slug argument cleans up that specific worktree without interactive selection (backward compat)
worktree-cleanup when no extra worktrees exist notifies the user and does not show selector
worktree-cleanup interactive mode allows selecting multiple worktrees
worktree-cleanup handles git worktree list failure gracefully
worktree extension fits repository structure conventions (name, commands shape)
ambiguousRequirements: Exact UI widget type for multi-select (the plan says 'pi inline thingy' referencing /ss command but does not specify the exact API contract for multi-select vs single-select)
Whether the main/current branch worktree should be excluded from the selection list or just not auto-selected
Whether cleanup means 'git worktree remove' alone or also involves branch deletion
notes: Tests use node:test + node:assert/strict to match all existing test examples in the repo.
Tests import worktreeExtension from ../../extensions/worktree.ts matching the existing import pattern.
The Ctx type and makeCtx helper mirror the pattern from the existing worktree test file (example 1).
All tests are behavioral: they exercise the actual worktree extension command handler, not markdown content.
Tests are designed to be RED: they will fail until the interactive selection feature is implemented in extensions/worktree.ts.
The backward-compatibility test ensures the existing slug-based cleanup still works after the new feature is added.
Edge cases cover: no worktrees to clean, git failure, and the structural convention check.
